user_query:
You are a senior digital workplace consultant onboarding a Fortune-500 client. You have been given a company profile. Your job is to design and build a complete, populated intranet hub — not a skeleton. A real internal workspace has density: multiple articles per channel, FAQs where they make sense, highlights, policies, leadership updates, and hub-wide polish. CRITICAL EXECUTION MODEL: - Phase 0 is the only planning/approval phase. - After the user approves Phase 0, the entire build is pre-approved. - Do not ask for confirmation again during Phases 1–5. - Do not ask whether to continue, whether to batch, or whether to publish remaining articles. - Do not stop after progress updates. In fact, do not give progress updates mid-build. - Continue tool-calling until the full hub is built, unless a tool returns an error. - If a tool fails, stop immediately and report what succeeded and what failed. COMPANY PROFILE: ---{company_profile}--- PHASE 0 — DEEP ANALYSIS & FULL DESIGN PROPOSAL (no tool calls yet) Read the profile TWICE. Then think about how this company ACTUALLY operate day-to-day. What announcements would leadership post? What policies must every employee acknowledge? What events, milestones, product launches are underway? What cultural rituals matter? Produce: 1. SCOPE CONFIG - Scope name (professional, matches brand voice) - Welcome message (1-2 sentences, specific to this company) - 5 brand hex colors (primary + contrast, secondary, warn + contrast) — use exact values from the profile if given - number_of_highlights (3-5), highlights_carousel (true), highlights_style - Footer: 3 columns with real links from the profile (About/Careers/Investors, Offices, Resources — adapt to the company) - Social links (derive from profile) 2. CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5-8 channels) Design channels that reflect how THIS company communicates. No generic names. Number each channel (Ch1, Ch2, ...) — this numbering is critical for Phase 3. For each channel provide: - Name (specific to the company's language) - Purpose (one sentence) - Type: public / private / corporate (corporate for leadership & compliance) - Color (hex — brand-aligned or function-coded: legal=red, hr=warm, ops=neutral) - Sticky: true for 1-2 critical channels only (Newsroom, Leadership) - Description: 2-3 sentences of real HTML, not placeholder text - Tabs: Articles-only for broadcast channels, Articles+Pages for reference channels - default_role: Reader for most, None for private/corporate - FAQ: 3-5 real Q&A pairs for HR, Legal, Compliance, IT channels. Skip FAQ on Newsroom/Leadership where it doesn't fit. 3. CONTENT CALENDAR (this is what makes the hub feel alive) Each PRIMARY channel gets 3-5 seeded articles. Secondary channels get 2-3. List articles grouped BY CHANNEL NUMBER. For every article propose: - Title (specific, not 'Welcome to X') - Channel it lives in - Type: announcement / policy / recap / spotlight / guide / milestone - 3-5 tags (e.g. ['q4-2024','earnings','leadership']) - is_main_highlight (pick 3-4 total across the whole hub) - is_sticky (per channel: 3-5 posts pinned) - require_read_confirmation (TRUE for policies, false for news) - status: Published for everything (this is a live hub) - notify: true for main announcements, false for evergreen content - One-sentence outline of what the body will cover ARTICLE IDEAS — derive these from the profile: • Recent financial results → quarterly recap with specific numbers • Product launches mentioned → launch recap / retrospective • ESG / sustainability commitments → progress update • Diversity / culture programs → employee resource group spotlight • Compliance / regulation → policy acknowledgement article • Office openings / regions → regional news • Leadership quotes → CEO letter or memo • R&D investments → innovation highlights 4. HUB-LEVEL POLISH - company_name, website_name (from profile) - default_language (infer from HQ: US→en-US, Portugal→pt-PT, Germany→de-DE) - supported_languages (list all operating regions) - login_quick_links: 3-5 useful public URLs from the profile (Careers, Investors, Newsroom, Support, etc.) - sidebar_navigation: ordered list emphasizing what this company values (e.g. collaborators first for a services firm, documents first for legal, etc.) Present this ENTIRE design proposal clearly in structured sections. Then ask: 'This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, article plan, colors, or hub settings?' STOP. Do not call any tool until the user approves. PHASE 1 — BUILD SCOPE (after design is approved) a. Call check_connection. b. Call create_scope with EVERY design field populated — no nulls, no defaults: name, welcome_message, channel_creation_by_admins_only=true, all 5 theme colors, number_of_highlights, highlights_carousel, highlights_style, footer_block_links (all 3 columns), navigation_links. c. Store the returned scope id as SCOPE_ID. d. Store the returned scope id as SCOPE_ID. Do not produce a user-facing message yet. Immediately proceed to Phase 2. PHASE 2 — BUILD CHANNELS with FAQs For each channel in the design, call create_channel with: - Full HTML description (never empty) - channel_type, color, is_sticky, tabs, initial_tab, default_role - frequent_questions populated for channels that have FAQ in the design - For leadership/newsroom-style channels, keep tabs=['Articles'] Save each returned channel id — you'll need them for Phase 3. After ALL channels are created, internally maintain this exact CHANNEL_MAP: CHANNEL_MAP: Ch1: [name] → [id] Ch2: [name] → [id] ... Do not print this as a final answer. Do not stop. Proceed immediately to Phase 3 once every channel ID is known. PHASE 3 — POPULATE CONTENT This phase must be completed without pausing. For every article in the approved content calendar: - Use the channel ID from CHANNEL_MAP. - Never guess or construct a channel ID. - Work through channels in order: finish all Ch1 articles, then Ch2, then Ch3, etc. - Call create_article for every planned article. - Store every created article ID returned by the tool. - Store the article IDs where is_main_highlight=true in HIGHLIGHT_ARTICLE_IDS. Do not ask whether to continue. Do not ask whether to batch. Do not produce progress updates. Do not stop after a channel is completed. Only stop if a tool returns an error. ARTICLE BODY CONTRACT: Each article body must be HTML and must include: - 450–650 words of actual content. - At least one

. - At least three

subsections. - At least six paragraphs. - At least one
    or
      . - At least one fact or phrase. - A closing CTA paragraph. - At least four concrete facts from the company profile where available. - No placeholder text. - No invented numbers. Before calling create_article, silently verify: - body is not empty; - body is not generic; - body has enough structure; - channel_id is from CHANNEL_MAP; - status is Published; - metadata matches the approved content calendar. If the article is a policy, compliance update, legal notice, security procedure, or required employee action, set require_read_confirmation=true. PHASE 3.5 — WIRE HOMEPAGE HIGHLIGHTS After all articles are published, wire the homepage highlights. Use the ordered list HIGHLIGHT_ARTICLE_IDS collected from articles where is_main_highlight=true. Important: - is_main_highlight=true alone is not enough. - The scope also needs its homepage highlight slots assigned. - If a dedicated set_scope_highlights tool is available, call it with: scope_id=SCOPE_ID article_ids=HIGHLIGHT_ARTICLE_IDS - If no dedicated tool exists but update_scope exposes additional_fields, call update_scope with the API's expected highlights payload. - If the highlight payload shape is unknown, report this clearly in the final handover: articles were marked highlight-eligible, but homepage slot wiring requires exposing the highlight-slot API in the MCP tools. Do not skip this phase silently. PHASE 4 — HUB-WIDE POLISH Call update_global_settings with EVERY field from the design: company_name, website_name, default_language, supported_languages, login_quick_links (3-5 real URLs), sidebar_navigation (ordered). This is what ties the hub together — skipping it breaks the illusion. PHASE 5 — FINAL HANDOVER Only now produce the final user-facing summary. Report: - Company - Scope name, id, route - Brand colors - Channels created with ids - Total articles published - Main highlight articles and whether homepage highlight slots were wired - Hub settings updated - Route Close with: "The workspace is live. Want to iterate on anything — rename a channel, add regional variants, seed more articles, or adjust branding?" HARD RULES — violating any of these breaks the demo • NEVER leave a description, welcome message, or article body null or empty. • NEVER use placeholder text ('Lorem ipsum', 'TODO', 'Welcome message here'). • NEVER fabricate facts not in the profile. If the profile doesn't say, reason plausibly or omit. Don't invent revenue numbers. • NEVER use generic channel names ('News', 'HR') if the profile suggests better ones (Apple Newsroom, People & Inclusion). • NEVER guess a channel id. Only use ids from CHANNEL_MAP or list_channels. • Every article body must have proper HTML structure, not just

      tags. • Every primary channel must have 3+ articles by the end of Phase 3. • If a tool call fails mid-build, stop, report what succeeded, ask how to proceed. • The finished workspace must feel built BY this company, FOR this company — not like a template with the name swapped in
